properties.remark: THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON SEVERAL ROADS IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK. A NUMBER OF SECONDARY DIRT ROADS WERE CLOSED. IN ADDITION...MUD AND ROCKS HAS WASHED ONTO PARTS OF BADWATER ROAD AND HIGHWAY 190 BETWEEN FURNACE CREEK AND COW CREEK AS WELL AS HIGHWAY 190 THROUGH THE PANAMINTS.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A SHADE HANGAR AT NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED VERY LOW VISIBILITY DUE TO BLOWING DUST LIKELY CONTRIBUTED TO SEVERAL VEHICLE ACCIDENTS. ONE SEMI-TRUCK WAS KNOCKED OVER NEAR NEWBERRY SPRINGS.
MINNEOLA ROAD WAS REPORTED CLOSED BY CALLIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL DUE TO ZERO VISIBILITY IN BLOWING DUST.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL HAS CLOSED I-40 FROM NATIONAL TRAILS HIGHWAY IN NEWBERRY SPRINGS TO THE DESERT OASIS REST AREA NEAR LUDLOW DUE TO LOW VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST. SEVERAL VEHICLE ACCIDENTS HAVE TAKEN PLACE IN THIS AREA LIKELY DUE TO THE LOW VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST. A FEW SEMIS HAVE GONE OFF THE ROAD AND FELL ON THEIR SIDE.

WATER 3 FEET DEEP FLOWING ACROSS DW RANCH ROAD. THE WATER FLOW CONTAINS ROCKS AND OTHER DEBRIS. AT 1130 DW RANCH ROAD WAS CLOSED BY THE MOHAVE COUNTY S.O. AT MILE MARKER 3 DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. ROAD IS CURRENTLY CLOSED.
THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW PRODUCED A 60 MPH WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHEAST AT THE KINGMAN AIRPORT.

*** 2 INJ *** BLOWING DUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY REDUCED THE VISIBILITY TO NEAR ZERO ON PIERCE FERRY ROAD FORCING THREE VEHICLES TO SLOW OR STOP. A FOURTH VEHICLE DROVE INTO THE DUST CLOUD AND STRUCK THE OTHER THREE VEHICLES. TWO PEOPLE HAD SERIOUS INJURIES AND HAD TO BE FLOWN BY MEDICAL HELICOPTER TO A HOSPITAL IN LAS VEGAS. NO OTHER INJURIES WERE REPORTED. ALL FOUR VEHICLES HAD TO BE REMOVED BY TOW TRUCKS.

*** 2 FATAL *** CHP REPORTS THAT A 3 VEHICLE ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON INTERSTATE 40 NEAR THE FORT CADY ROAD EXIT. THIS ACCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY REDUCED VISIBILITY ASSOCIATED WITH BLOWING DUST. TWO PEOPLE WERE KILLED IN THE ACCIDENT. INTERSTATE 40 WAS CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS EAST OF FORT CADY ROAD DUE TO REDUCED VISIBILITY FOR SEVERAL HOURS. UPDATED LSR TO INCLUDE FATALITIES.

CHP REPORTS THAT A 3 VEHICLE ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON INTERSTATE 40 NEAR THE FORT CADY ROAD EXIT. THIS ACCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY REDUCED VISIBILITY ASSOCIATED WITH BLOWING DUST. INTERSTATE 40 WAS CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS EAST OF FORT CADY ROAD DUE TO REDUCED VISIBILITY FOR SEVERAL HOURS.

*** 1 FATAL *** A 77 YEAR OLD FEMALE WAS KILLED WHEN THE WHITE 2009 HYUNDAI SONATA SHE WAS DRIVING WAS SWEPT AWAY BY FLASH FLOODING. THE CAR WAS TRAVELING ALONG US 95 NORTH OF INTERSTATE 40 WHEN IT ENCOUNTERED FLOWING WATER AT THE SACRAMENTO WASH. US 95 WAS CLOSED FROM INTERSTATE 40 TO THE NV LINE AFTER THE INCIDENT DUE TO ROADWAY FLOODING. UPDATED TO ADD DETAILS OF THE FATALITY.
INTERSTATE 40 IS CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS AT US 95 DUE TO FLOODING.

ABOUT 12 DIFFERENT LOCATIONS ALONG THE BNSF RAILWAY LINE WERE COVERED IN ROCKS AND MUD NEAR NEEDLES. RUSHING WATER IN THE SACRAMENTO WASH TOOK OUT SUPPORT FOR A RAILROAD BRIDGE AND STRANDED TWO FREIGHT TRAINS. APPROXIMATELY 2.6 MILES OF RAILROAD LINE WAS DAMAGED. SOME RAILROAD PERSONNEL WERE STUCK ON THE BRIDGE AND HAD TO BE RESCUED. FREIGHT LINE AND AMTRACK SERVICE WAS SUSPENDED AS A RESULT.
SMALL ROCKS WASHED ONTO THE FAR WEST END OF LONE MOUNTAIN ROAD AS WELL AS THE FAR WEST END OF ANN ROAD. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER IS FLOWING DOWN BRENT LANE AT FT APACHE ROAD.

ALMOST ALL SURFACE STREETS IN NORTHWEST LAS VEGAS HAD MUD AND ROCKS WASH ON THEM. FARM ROAD WAS CLOSED FROM TENAYA WAY TO JONES. OTHER ROADS THAT HAD MUD AND ROCKS WASH ONTO THEM INCLUDED PORTIONS OF NORTH DURANGO...FORT APACHE...EL CAPITAN AND CLIFF SHADOWS DRIVE.

ALMOST ALL ROADS IN THE ALIANTE AREA OF NORTH LAS VEGAS WERE COVERED IN MUD AND MANY HAD ROCKS WASH ONTO THEM. MUD COVERED SOME AREAS TO THE DEPTH OF A FEW INCHES. A NWS EMPLOYEE MEASURED 1.48 INCHES OF RAIN FOR A STORM TOTAL...MOST OF WHICH FELL IN LESS THAN 90 MINUTES. PORTIONS OF A PARKING LOT AT AN APARTMENT COMPLEX WERE COVERED IN 3 TO 6 INCHES OF STANDING WATER STRANDING SOME VEHICLES. THE ROOF WAS LEAKING AT THE WAL-MART. FLOODING WAS CONSIDERED THE WORST SINCE THIS AREA WAS FIRST BUILT.

DEBRIS FLOW FROM THE CARPENTER 1 FIRE BURN AREA AFFECTED A LARGE AREA OF NORTHWEST LAS VEGAS FROM NEAR HUALAPAI AND GRAND TETON TO RAINBOW AND GRAND TETON. MANY AREAS ALONG GRAND TETON WERE COVERED IN AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER...MUD AND EVEN FOAM LEFT FROM FIREFIGHTING EFFORTS FOR THE CARPENTER 1 FIRE. PORTIONS OF GRAND TETON WERE CLOSED. DAMAGE ESTIMATES TO ROADS ARE OVER A MILLION DOLLARS.
